content.body: Trigonometry is the study of relationships between angles and sides in triangles. The three basic trigonometric ratios — sine, cosine, and tangent — relate the angles of a right-angled triangle to the lengths of its sides.

In a right-angled triangle:
• Hypotenuse: longest side, opposite the right angle
• Opposite: side facing the angle θ
• Adjacent: side next to angle θ (not hypotenuse)

SOH-CAH-TOA:
Sin θ = Opposite / Hypotenuse
Cos θ = Adjacent / Hypotenuse
Tan θ = Opposite / Adjacent

content.title: Finding a Side Length
content.body:
A right triangle has angle θ = 30° and hypotenuse = 10 cm. Find the opposite side.
Using SOH: sin 30° = Opposite / 10
0.5 = Opposite / 10
Opposite = 10 × 0.5 = 5 cm

content.title: Finding an Angle
content.body:
A right triangle has opposite = 6 cm and hypotenuse = 10 cm.
sin θ = 6/10 = 0.6
θ = sin⁻¹(0.6) = 36.87° ≈ 37°

Special Angles (memorise these!):
Angle: 0°   30°   45°   60°   90°
Sin:   0    1/2   √2/2  √3/2  1
Cos:   1    √3/2  √2/2  1/2   0
Tan:   0    1/√3  1     √3    undefined

questions.question: SOH-CAH-TOA means:
questions.explanation: SOH: Sin=Opposite/Hypotenuse, CAH: Cos=Adjacent/Hypotenuse, TOA: Tan=Opposite/Adjacent.

questions.question: What is sin 30°?
questions.explanation: sin 30° = 0.5 = 1/2. A standard value to memorise.

questions.question: What is cos 60°?
A right triangle: opposite=5, hypotenuse=13. Find sin θ.
What is tan 45°?
questions.explanation: tan 45° = 1. At 45°, opposite = adjacent, so their ratio is 1.

questions.question: angle θ = 40°, adjacent = 8 cm. Find the hypotenuse using cos.
questions.explanation: cos θ = Adj/Hyp, so Hyp = Adj/cos θ = 8/cos 40° ≈ 10.44 cm.

questions.question: Which ratio uses the opposite and adjacent sides?
sin θ = 0.8. Find θ.
A ladder of length 5 m leans against a wall at 60°. How high up the wall does it reach?
What is cos 0°?
questions.explanation: cos 0° = 1. As the angle approaches 0, the adjacent approaches the hypotenuse.

content.body:
Pythagoras' Theorem (right-angled triangles only):
a² + b² = c²
Where c is the hypotenuse (longest side)
a and b are the two shorter sides (legs)

content.title: Finding the Hypotenuse
content.body:
A right triangle has legs a=3 and b=4. Find the hypotenuse.
c² = 3² + 4² = 9 + 16 = 25
c = √25 = 5

This is the famous 3-4-5 Pythagorean triple!

content.title: Finding a Leg
content.body:
Hypotenuse = 13 cm, one leg = 5 cm. Find the other leg.
a² + 5² = 13²
a² + 25 = 169
a² = 144
a = √144 = 12 cm
Common Pythagorean triples to remember: (3,4,5), (5,12,13), (8,15,17), (7,24,25). These ratios also work as multiples — e.g. (6,8,10), (9,12,15).

content.title: Real-World Application
content.body:
A football pitch is 100m long and 64m wide. What is the diagonal distance corner-to-corner?
d² = 100² + 64² = 10000 + 4096 = 14096
d = √14096 ≈ 118.7 m

questions.question: State Pythagoras' theorem for a right triangle with hypotenuse c:
questions.explanation: Pythagoras: a² + b² = c² where c is the hypotenuse.

questions.question: Legs are 5 and 12. Find the hypotenuse.
Hypotenuse=10, one leg=6. Find the other leg.
Is (6, 8, 10) a Pythagorean triple?
questions.explanation: 6²+8²=36+64=100=10². Yes, it is a valid Pythagorean triple (double of 3-4-5).

questions.question: A right triangle has legs 9 and 40. What is the hypotenuse?
Pythagoras' theorem applies to:
questions.explanation: Pythagoras' theorem only works for triangles with a 90° angle.

questions.question: Hypotenuse=25, one leg=7. Find the other leg.
A 10m ladder reaches a wall at 8m height. How far is the base from the wall?
In the formula a²+b²=c², which side is c?
questions.explanation: c is always the hypotenuse — the side opposite the right angle and the longest side.

questions.question: Legs are both 1 unit. What is the hypotenuse?
